linter_checks.description: Test function contains no pytest assertions.
Assertion is always true, such as assert True or assert X or True.
Only assertion checks returncode == 0.
Assertion allows non-zero exits with returncode in a list.
Test body is just pass.
Assertion uses A or B instead of asserting an exact outcome.
If branch asserts with no else, silently passing when false.
Both if and else branches assert, suggesting non-deterministic behavior.
Except handler with pass swallows failures.
All assertions only check returncode, len, or isdigit.
Substring check is shorter than 15 characters.
Test writes to its own golden file.
Golden file is referenced in docstring but never compared with ==.
Golden file is mentioned in docstring but not found in the test body.
Loop-only assertions have no pre-loop length check.
Sole assertion is len(x) > N.
Assertion uses len(x) >= N, a relative bound that verifies no content.
any()/all() assertion has no non-empty guard.
path.exists() assertion has no content assertion.
All assertions are negative checks such as not in or !=.
Missing or too-short CATCHES docstring.

checks.evidence: task_env contains only executable, compile.sh, and docs.
USAGE.md names the CLI, language, standard-library, error, and runtime boundaries covered by hidden tests.
USAGE.md forbids binary/source shortcuts and limits observation to CLI behavior.
No local workspace, source, gold, repository URL, or commit leaks in pytest wrappers.
Test wrappers avoid direct subprocess/source inspection and route behavior through the run_pocket fixture.
Wrappers assert exact return code, stdout, and stderr instead of broad substring checks.
23 harvested cases and 396 generated cases have exact outputs, unique ids, no local path leaks, and non-empty observable behavior.
Gold passed 421 tests with 0 failures; dummy passed 0 and failed 421.
Harvested coverage 68.0% line/59.6% branch; generated coverage 72.7% line/62.3% branch.

findings.finding: Deterministic hidden-test fairness audit passes for the PocketLang generated suite and checks black-box test structure plus documentation sufficiency.
The solver-facing PocketLang task_env contains only compile.sh, docs, and the oracle executable; hidden tests and source are outside the solve container.
Generated tests are exact-output behavioral tests that run the candidate executable via pytest wrappers and reject a dummy implementation.
The hardened evaluator compiles offline with --network none, removes prebuilt executables, rejects gold hash/embedded oracle bytes, and scored an empty stub at 0/421.
Solver-facing docs identify PocketLang 0.1.0 behavior, the offline compile contract, the oracle observation boundary, and the end-to-end behavior surface.
No PocketLang mini-SWE solve trajectory exists yet; trajectory-specific fairness checks should be rerun after the first solve/eval.
